question_text: A light bulb converts 60 J of electrical energy into 12 J of light energy. The rest is wasted as heat. What is the efficiency of the light bulb?
explanation: Efficiency = useful energy output ÷ total energy input × 100%. Here, useful output is 12 J and total input is 60 J, so 12 ÷ 60 × 100 = 20%. This means only one-fifth of the energy becomes light; the rest becomes wasted heat.
